title: Apply for a BIDC certificate of origin
description: Apply for a Barbados Investment and Development Corporation certificate of origin for any export.
summary: Some exports do not fit under one of the CARICOM trade agreements – for example, sales to countries with no specific trade agreement, or sales of products that are not covered by an agreement. For these, the Barbados Investment and Development Corporation issues a general certificate of origin.

The certificate confirms that the goods were made in Barbados. It can be useful for customs in the destination country, for the buyer’s records, or to claim that goods are eligible for general-system-of-preferences (GSP) rates.

Manufacturer details go in Part A. Exporter details follow if the exporter is not the same as the manufacturer. You will also need to confirm the goods, the consignee, the country of destination and the transport route.
Bring an invoice and a packing list, plus proof that the goods were actually made in Barbados (for example, a manufacturer’s declaration or a list of materials and processes).

items: the manufacturer’s name, address, phone and email
details of the goods (description, packages, weight, value)
the country of origin and destination

items.name: Bill of lading or airway bill
Proof that the goods were made in Barbados

how_long: Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Application for Certificate of Origin (Part A: Manufacturer Information) to BIDC at Pelican House.

contact.address:
Pelican House, Princess Alice Highway
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days’ is an estimate.
‘Eligible for GSP rates’ is one possible use but not the only one. The certificate is general-purpose.
